title: Logistics at the North Pole (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 5). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if the counting array is the wrong size, base the frequency counting marks on whether the student increments the correct index consistently.
This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. The Java method is calc(int[] A, int[] B); the Python function is calc(A, B). Both are correct.
The substrings 'sort', 'import' (Python), and 'numpy' must not appear in the solution. Penalise in point 5.
The algorithm must run in O(n + m) time. Solutions using nested loops or sorting are not acceptable.

rubric_points.name: Counting array allocation
rubric_points.target: A counting array C of size at least max(A)+1 (equivalently |B| or 10^6+1) is allocated and initialised to 0.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No counting array is allocated; the algorithm attempts to use nested loops, sorting, or direct comparison without accumulating frequencies.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A counting array is present but has the wrong size (e.g., size n instead of |B| or 10^6+1), which would cause index-out-of-bounds for valid inputs, or the array is not initialised to 0.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: A counting array C of size at least max(A)+1 is allocated and initialised to 0, allowing each category in A to be counted by direct indexing.

rubric_points.name: Frequency counting over A
rubric_points.target: For each element a in A, C[a] is incremented by 1, accumulating the count of each category.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: A is not iterated; no frequencies are counted, or the loop iterates over B instead of A.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A is iterated but the wrong index is incremented: for example, C[i]++ uses the loop index i instead of the element value C[A[i]]++, or counts are overwritten rather than accumulated (= instead of +=).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: For each element a in A, C[a] is incremented by 1, correctly accumulating the frequency of each category across the entire array.

rubric_points.name: Output loop bounds
rubric_points.target: The output loop iterates i from 0 to |B|-1 inclusive, producing a binary string of exactly length |B| = m.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Output loop iterates over A (length n) instead of B (length m), producing a string of the wrong length.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Output loop iterates over B but with incorrect bounds (e.g., 1 to |B| instead of 0 to |B|-1), producing a string that is one character short or contains an off-by-one error.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Output loop iterates i from 0 to |B|-1 inclusive, producing a binary string of exactly length m.

rubric_points.name: Comparison and output character
rubric_points.target: For each position i, '1' is appended when C[i] <= B[i] (category fits in one pass), and '0' when C[i] > B[i].

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No comparison between C[i] and B[i] is made; all positions output the same character regardless of counts and capacities.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: C[i] and B[i] are compared but the direction is reversed: '1' is output when C[i] > B[i] and '0' when C[i] <= B[i], inverting the intended result.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: '1' is appended when C[i] <= B[i] and '0' when C[i] > B[i], correctly indicating whether all items of category i fit within the sleigh capacity.

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: The method returns the correct binary string of length m for all valid inputs in O(n + m) time, without forbidden substrings.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Returns an incorrect string for most inputs, or contains forbidden substrings ('sort', 'import', 'numpy').

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Returns correct output for simple inputs but fails for edge cases (e.g., categories with 0 items, B[i] == 0), or the algorithm is O(n*m) due to nested loops.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns the correct binary string for all valid inputs in O(n + m) time; forbidden substrings are absent.
